organizations.label: European AI Office
organizations.description: The European Commission body responsible for implementing and enforcing the AI Act's rules for general-purpose AI models at Union level.
organizations.notes: Its authority arises from the AI Act; supporting guidance and the voluntary GPAI Code do not independently create equivalent legal obligations.

organizations.label: United States National Institute of Standards and Technology
organizations.description: A United States public standards and measurement body that develops technical AI risk-management guidance and evaluation resources.
organizations.notes: The AI Risk Management Framework is voluntary and is not treated as binding regulatory authority.

organizations.label: United Kingdom AI Security Institute
organizations.description: A United Kingdom government research organization that develops and conducts evaluations of advanced AI systems.
organizations.notes: It is represented as a technical evaluation body, not a regulator or enforcement authority.

organizations.label: Cyberspace Administration of China
organizations.description: China's national cyberspace authority and one of the issuing bodies for binding rules governing generative AI services.

organizations.label: OECD.AI
organizations.description: The OECD's policy platform for comparing AI principles, policies, institutions, and national approaches.
organizations.notes: It supplies comparative policy context and voluntary principles, not enforcement authority.

organizations.label: OpenAI
organizations.description: A private developer and deployer of general-purpose and frontier AI models with operational control over model development and access.
organizations.notes: Evidence assignment is deferred to the substantive coding stage.

organizations.label: Anthropic
organizations.description: A private developer and deployer of general-purpose and frontier AI models with operational control over model development and access.
organizations.notes: Evidence assignment is deferred to the substantive coding stage.

organizations.label: Google DeepMind
organizations.description: A private developer and deployer of general-purpose and frontier AI models with operational control over model development and access.
organizations.notes: Evidence assignment is deferred to the substantive coding stage.
